findings.citation: process: no telemetry entry in /home/ubuntu/gt-town/.runtime/refinery-telemetry/refinery-gate-20260624.jsonl; no gate log in /home/ubuntu/gt-town/.runtime/refinery-gate-logs/ for 664aaf60
findings.explanation: Bypass-merge: commit 664aaf60 reached origin/main with zero gate telemetry. The committer date is 2026-06-24T15:39:50-04:00 = 2026-06-24T19:39:50Z; the surrounding gate logs are 19:10:04Z and 19:59:12Z, with no entry between them. There is no event for 664aaf60 in refinery-gate-20260624.jsonl (295 lines, grep returns no matches). Per the strict four-model rule (m3 / codex / umans-kimi / umans-glm) established in gastown-cet.12.4, every merge to main must have a recorded gate panel. This is strictly worse than the gastown-cet.12.4 degraded-quorum case (which at least had partial telemetry). Follow-up: file bead to require a CI failure when a commit reaches main without a gate record, and to investigate the refinery pipeline for the 2026-06-24T19:10Z-19:59Z gap.

findings.citation: commit message 'for new gtviz rig' vs diff in /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gastown/.beads/config.yaml
findings.explanation: Misleading commit message: the subject says 'add explicit shared Dolt config keys for new gtviz rig' but the change is in the GASTOWN root .beads/config.yaml. The gtviz rig did not exist at the time of the commit (gtviz .beads/config.yaml was created 2026-06-30T19:58:43-04:00 per stat(1), six days after this commit landed at 2026-06-24T19:39:50Z). The change actually repairs the gastown root's own dolt-config doctor-check, not gtviz's. The commit message does not say this. Follow-up: file bead to require commit-message scope review (and have the merge-queue reject commits whose subject says 'for X rig' but whose diff is in a different rig's tree).

findings.citation: gtviz bootstrap (2026-06-30T19:58:43-04:00) wrote the same three keys independently
findings.explanation: Redundant work for gtviz: the gtviz rig's own bootstrap (six days after this commit) independently wrote storage.backend: dolt + dolt.server: 127.0.0.1 + dolt.port: 3307 to /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gtviz/.beads/config.yaml. The change in 664aaf60 was effectively a no-op for the gtviz rig — its only real effect is on the gastown root's doctor-check, which the commit message does not acknowledge. Follow-up: file bead to document the relationship (gastown root config was made explicit first; gtviz bootstrap inherited the same convention).

No tests added. The change is to a key the doctor check requires; no test in internal/doctor/dolt_config_check_test.go was updated to assert the gastown-root key set is recognized. Follow-up: file bead to add a test that asserts the gastown-root .beads/config.yaml key set is honored by dolt_config_check.go::checkBeadsDir.

Inconsistent quoting: dolt.server: "127.0.0.1" is quoted (because YAML scalar 127.0.0.1 could be ambiguously parsed) but dolt.port: 3307 is bare. The doctor check parser (configYAMLValues at internal/doctor/dolt_config_check.go:204) trims quotes, so both work, but the asymmetry is jarring. The pre-existing config style also mixes (some bare, some quoted), so this is a low-priority cleanup. Follow-up: include in any rework commit.

summary: FAIL — Even after v1's two wrong BLOCKING findings (wrong file, port coupling) are dropped (v1 had pointed to /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gtviz/.repo.git/.beads/config.yaml and claimed gtviz uses a different port; v2 verifies F1 the active gtviz beads dir is /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gtviz/.beads/ and F2 gtviz uses port 3307), the change is still a strict bypass-merge. The single remaining BLOCKING is the zero-gate-telemetry finding: no event in refinery-gate-20260624.jsonl, no gate log at the 19:39:50Z landing time, no evidence any reviewer was invoked. The change is also misleadingly scoped (commit says 'for gtviz rig' but the diff is in gastown root) and effectively redundant for gtviz (gtviz bootstrap later wrote the same keys). Required: investigate the refinery pipeline for the 2026-06-24T19:10Z-19:59Z gap and require a gate record for any future merge to main.
